zh.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir是William Knapp和Jyllian Jane Knapp的兒子，於1503年出生在英國英格蘭波克夏阿爾德沃斯。他於1521年與Lady Margaret Francis在英國英格蘭諾福爾可守爾達哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1555年之前在英國波克夏波福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir是William Knapp和Jyllian Jane Knapp的儿子，于1503年出生在英国英格兰波克夏阿爾德沃斯。他于1521年与Lady Margaret Francis在英国英格兰諾福爾可守爾達哈姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1555年之前在英国波克夏波福德。

nameMeanings.name: Knapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: occupational or status name from the German word Knapp(e), a variant of Knabe ‘young unmarried man’. In the 15th century this spelling acquired the separate, specialized meanings ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovakia, where it is more commonly spelled Knap (compare 3 below).
German: in Franconia, a nickname for a dexterous or skillful person, of the same ultimate origin as 1 above.
Germanized or Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian   Knap  , a surname of ultimately German origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Knappe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus, Rainer, Siegfried, Wolf.
German: variant of   Knapp  .

variants.name: Knap
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn, and Slovenian: of German origin (see   Knapp   1), meaning e.g. ‘pageboy, armor bearer’ (Czech), ‘weaver’ (Polish, Slovak, and Rusyn), ‘wool comber’ (Slovak and Slovenian), and ‘miner’ (Slovenian and Czech).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Knapp   6.

variants.name: Knabe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Gerhard, Helmut, Volker.

German: status name for a young man or a page, from Middle High German knabe. In aristocratic circles this term denoted a page or squire (a youth destined to become a knight), while among artisans it referred to a journeyman's assistant or (as a short form of Lehrknabe) ‘apprentice’. In the 15th century a semantic split between Knabe and its variants Knape, Knapp(e) resulted in Knabe meaning ‘boy’ and Knapp(e) ‘servant, apprentice’, and ‘miner’ in modern German.

variants.name: Canup
meanings: Americanized form of North German   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knupp
meanings: German: variant of   Knapp   or   Knopp  .

variants.name: Knibbs
meanings: English: variant, with post-medieval excrescent -s, of Knibb, which may be related to Knapp or Nobbs or Knipe .

variants.name: Knapper
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English knap(p)er(e), an agent derivative of knap(pe) ‘knob, hillock’, for someone who lived by a hilltop or hillock; compare Knapp .
German (also Knäpper): habitational name for someone from either of two places in Westphalia named Knapp.
German (Knäpper): unflattering nickname from an agent derivative of knappen ‘to be stingy’ or, in some places, ‘to grab or snatch’.

variants.name: Knapke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Florian.
German: from a pet form of   Knapp  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir was born in 1503, in Aldworth, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William Knapp and Jyllian Jane Knapp. He married Lady Margaret Francis in 1521, in Shouldham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died before 1555, in Boxford, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir est né en 1503, à Aldworth, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William Knapp et Jyllian Jane Knapp. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Francis en 1521, à Shouldham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé avant 1555, à Boxford, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir wurde 1503 in Aldworth, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William Knapp und Jyllian Jane Knapp geboren. Er hat 1521 in Shouldham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Lady Margaret Francis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb vor 1555 in Boxford, Berkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir è nato il nel 1503 nel Aldworth, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di William Knapp e Jyllian Jane Knapp. Lui sposato Lady Margaret Francis nel 1521, nel Shouldham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì prima del 1555, nel Boxford, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sirは1503年にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアアルドウオルスでWilliam KnappとJyllian Jane Knappの息子として生まれた。1521年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクショウルドハムでLady Margaret Francisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1555年以前にイギリスバークシュアイアボクスフォードで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir(은)는 1503년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 알드워스에서 William Knapp(와)과 Jyllian Jane Knapp의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1521년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 쇼우드함에서 Lady Margaret Francis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1555년 이전 영국 버크실르 보스퍼드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Knapp Sir родился в 1503 г. в местности алдворт, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали William Knapp и Jyllian Jane Knapp. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Francis в 1521 г. в местности шоулдхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер перед 1555 г. в местности боксфорд, биркшир, Англия.
